subject: Conditional Probability and Independence
question: An actuary studied the likelihood that different types of drivers would be involved in at least one collision during any one-year period. The results of the study are presented below. Type of driver / Percentage of all drivers / Probability of at least one collision: Teen, 8%, 0.15; Young adult, 16%, 0.08; Midlife, 45%, 0.04; Senior, 31%, 0.05; Total, 100%. Given that a driver has been involved in at least one collision in the past year, what is the probability that the driver is a young adult driver?
